response.justification: Bogdan is a technically broad Staff-level engineer with directly relevant experience in AI-agent infrastructure and agentic workflows at re:cinq, plus proven high-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests per second). However, Python — a stated must-have — is entirely absent from his skills and employment history, which is a significant gap. His TypeScript depth is strong, and his current AI-native work is a genuine differentiator, but the Python gap and limited explicit evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management warrant further screening before committing to an interview.
strengths: Current hands-on work building AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq — directly addresses the role's AI-native baseline expectation
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O and high-performance caching
Consistent Staff-level tenure across multiple organizations with technical breadth spanning TypeScript, distributed architecture, event-driven systems, and developer tooling
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume — skills list, job descriptions, and projects — despite being listed as a must-have for the role
Cross-functional stakeholder management and bridging engineering/product/leadership perspectives are not evidenced anywhere in the resume
The strongest distributed systems work (Dunnhumby) is six-plus years old and lasted only ~22 months; more recent roles are heavier on product engineering than large-scale infrastructure
